tidyllm 0.5.0.9000

    • Classifying Texts with tidyllm

    • Structured Question Answering from PDFs
  • Changelog

tidyllm

Source: claude.md

A tidy, functional interface for large language model APIs in R.

Package Identity

tidyllm provides a pipeline-oriented, functional interface for working with LLM APIs in data analysis workflows. Unlike agent-focused packages, tidyllm emphasizes:

  • Batch processing for cost-efficient large-scale LLM operations
  • Provider-specific features that other packages consider out-of-scope
  • Immutable message objects for pipeline safety
  • Tidy data outputs (tibbles) for seamless integration with tidyverse workflows

Relationship to ellmer: complementary, not competitive. tidyllm targets data pipelines, batch operations, and provider-specific power features. ellmer targets interactive agents, Shiny apps, and async use cases.

Core Architecture

The Verb + Provider Pattern

All API interactions follow: verb(.llm, provider())

llm_message("Hello") |> chat(openai())
c("text1", "text2") |> embed(openai())
list_of_messages |> send_batch(claude())

Verbs: chat(), embed(), send_batch(), check_batch(), fetch_batch(), list_batches(), list_models(), deep_research(), check_job(), fetch_job(), upload_file(), list_files(), file_info(), delete_file()

  • check_job() / fetch_job(): type-dispatching aliases — delegate to check_batch()/fetch_batch() for batch objects (have batch_id attr), or to provider-specific research functions for tidyllm_research_job objects (dispatches on job$provider: "openai" → openai_check_research/openai_fetch_research; "perplexity" → perplexity_check_research/perplexity_fetch_research)
  • upload_file() / list_files() / file_info() / delete_file(): remote file verbs — manage files stored on provider servers (Claude, Gemini, OpenAI); other providers error with a clear message

Providers: openai(), claude(), gemini(), ollama(), mistral(), groq(), perplexity(), deepseek(), voyage(), azure_openai(), openrouter(), llamacpp(), chat_completions(), mixedbread() (planned)

Batch providers (support send/check/fetch/list): Claude, OpenAI, Mistral, Groq, Gemini

Deep research providers: Perplexity (sonar-deep-research via async endpoint); OpenAI (o3-deep-research, o4-mini-deep-research via Responses API background mode)

How Verb Dispatch Works

create_provider_function() creates each provider function. When called from a verb, the verb: 1. Evaluates the provider expression to get the call 2. Calls provider(.called_from = "metadata") to get supported argument names 3. Filters common args to only those supported by this provider 4. Injects them via rlang::call_modify and evals the result

This means chat(openai(), .thinking = TRUE) correctly errors because .thinking is not in openai_chat’s formals. Each provider’s chat function is the authoritative source of what it supports.

The shared dispatch_to_provider(provider_expr, verb_name, common_args, validate) helper in llm_verbs.R implements this logic once — all verbs delegate to it. Use validate = FALSE for verbs like check_batch()/fetch_batch()/list_batches() that don’t need metadata lookup.

Key Classes (S7)

  • LLMMessage: Immutable conversation history container. History stored as a list of message objects in @message_history. Each message has content, media (inline media: base64-encoded binary read from disk at message construction time), and files (remote file references: tidyllm_file objects pointing to files stored on a provider’s servers).
  • APIProvider: Base class with @short_name, @long_name, @api_key_env_var. Provides default implementations for most generics.
  • TOOL: Tool/function definition. Has @func (R function), @input_schema (named list of tidyllm_field), @builtin (for provider-native tools like Claude web search).
  • tidyllm_field: Schema field descriptor with @type, @description, @enum, @vector, @schema (for nested objects).
  • tidyllm_image: Inline image; returned by img(). Base64-encoded eagerly at construction; stored in message$media; provider-agnostic.
  • tidyllm_audio: Inline audio; returned by audio_file(). Stores path and MIME only; binary read and base64-encode deferred to to_api_format().
  • tidyllm_video: Inline video; returned by video_file(). Same lazy-encoding pattern as audio.
  • tidyllm_pdf: Inline PDF; returned by pdf_file(). Stores path + pdftools text extraction at construction; to_api_format() uses binary where supported, silently falls back to tagged text otherwise.
  • tidyllm_file: Remote file reference; returned by upload_file(). Has @id, @provider, @mime_type, @filename, @uri. Stored in message$files. Provider-specific: a Claude file cannot be sent to Gemini; mismatch is validated at to_api_format() time.

File Organization

R/
├── LLMMessage.R           # LLMMessage class, add/remove message generics
├── APIProvider.R          # Provider base class and all generics
├── llm_verbs.R            # Verb dispatch (chat, embed, send_batch, etc.) + create_provider_function()
├── llm_message.R          # llm_message() constructor
├── message_retrieval.R    # get_reply(), get_metadata(), get_reply_data()
├── tidyllm_schema.R       # JSON schema system (tidyllm_field, field_*(), tidyllm_schema())
├── tools.R                # TOOL class, tidyllm_tool(), ellmer_tool(), tools_to_api generics
├── perform_api_requests.R # perform_generic_request(), perform_chat_request(), perform_embedding_request()
├── media.R                # tidyllm_image, tidyllm_file (S7); img(), audio_file(), video_file(), pdf_file(); extract_media()
├── rate_limits.R          # Per-provider rate limit tracking from response headers
├── embedding_helpers.R    # Shared embedding utilities
├── pdfbatch.R             # PDF batch processing utilities
├── utilites.R             # validate_inputs(), %||%, other helpers
├── api_chat_completions.R # ChatCompletions base class (renamed from api_openai); shared by 7 providers
├── api_openai.R           # OpenAI Responses API (POST /v1/responses); extends APIProvider directly
├── api_claude.R           # Claude chat + extended thinking + file uploads + batch
├── api_gemini.R           # Gemini chat + video/audio + batch
├── api_groq.R             # Groq chat + batch
├── api_mistral.R          # Mistral chat + batch
├── api_ollama.R           # Ollama local models
├── api_perplexity.R       # Perplexity search/reasoning
├── api_deepseek.R         # DeepSeek models
├── api_voyage.R           # Voyage embeddings
├── api_azure_openai.R     # Azure OpenAI deployments
├── api_ellmer.R           # chat_ellmer() — use ellmer Chat objects as provider
├── api_openrouter.R       # OpenRouter — chat + embed + list_models; access to 300+ models via one key
└── api_llamacpp.R         # llama.cpp local server — chat + embed + list_models + BNF grammar + logprobs + model management
└── zzz.R / tidyllm-package.R

Working with tidyllm Code

S7 Patterns

# Class definition
MyClass <- new_class("MyClass", properties = list(
  prop1 = class_character,
  prop2 = class_list
))

# Generic definition (dispatch on multiple args)
my_generic <- new_generic("my_generic", c(".api", ".input"))

# Method implementation
method(my_generic, list(api_openai, class_list)) <- function(.api, .input) {
  # implementation
}

# Property access
obj@property_name

Important: Always consult S7 documentation before modifying S7 code. S7 is a recent R OOP system and many LLMs have limited knowledge of it.

Key Generics to Implement per Provider

Defined in APIProvider.R. Defaults exist for most; override as needed:

Generic Dispatches on Purpose
to_api_format() .api, .llm Convert LLMMessage to provider message format
parse_chat_response() .api, .response Extract assistant reply from API JSON
extract_metadata() .api, .response Extract token counts, model, cost
handle_stream() .api, .response Process SSE streaming response
extract_metadata_stream() .api, .raw_data Extract metadata from streaming data
tools_to_api() .api, .tools Convert TOOL list to provider’s tool schema
run_tool_calls() .api, .tool_calls, .tools Execute tool calls, return results
send_tool_results() .api, .request, .body Send tool results back to provider
ratelimit_from_header() .api, .headers Parse rate limit info from headers
has_tool_calls() .api, .response Check if response contains pending tool calls
extract_tool_calls() .api, .response Extract tool call objects from response
append_tool_messages() .api, .request_body, .response, .tool_results Append tool messages to request body for next round

Adding a New Provider

  1. Create R/api_newprovider.R

  2. Define S7 class: api_newprovider <- new_class("api_newprovider", APIProvider)

  3. Implement required generics (at minimum: to_api_format, parse_chat_response, extract_metadata)

  4. Write newprovider_chat() function following existing provider patterns

  5. Register via create_provider_function():

    newprovider <- create_provider_function(
  .name = "newprovider",
  chat = newprovider_chat,
  embed = newprovider_embedding   # if supported
)

Code Style

  • Prefix internal arguments with . (e.g., .llm, .model, .api)

  • Use validate_inputs() for argument validation — named vector pattern:

    c("Error msg" = condition1, "Other msg" = condition2) |> validate_inputs()

  • Return tibbles for all tabular data

  • Functions should be pure when possible — no side effects

  • Do not add code comments unless specifically requested

  • No trailing summaries after tool calls

  • No em-dashes in prose or documentation. Use semicolons or comma appositions instead (e.g. “aliases; they work on…” or “embeddings, meaning text and images…”). In definition lists use a colon.

Testing

Local Tests (preferred for this package)

Live-API package. Prefer real non-mocked local tests. Keep them in local_tests/.

What goes where: - Mocked tests (tests/testthat/): basic chat + embed shape checks only. CRAN-safe. - Local tests (local_tests/): everything live — tool use, streaming, async, structured output, file uploads, provider-specific features. Tool use and async must stay here.

Structure:

local_tests/
├── run_tests.R              # run_all_local_tests(providers=NULL); run_feature_tests()
├── test_harness.R           # llt_test(), llt_expect_*(), llt_report()
├── async_tracker.R          # save/load/check async job state; job_is_pending()
├── batch_submit.R           # opt-in: submit plain + json_schema batches per provider (idempotent)
├── batch_fetch.R            # opt-in: fetch completed batch jobs and assert results
├── start_llamacpp_servers.sh  # start chat (8080) + embed (8081) servers; idempotent
├── media/                   # Test assets (images, PDFs, audio) — tracked in git
├── providers/               # Per-provider suites — source one after changing a provider
│   ├── claude.R             # chat, stream, tools, thinking (no batch — moved to batch_submit/fetch)
│   ├── openai.R             # chat, stream, tools, vision, reasoning_effort, stateful, built-in tools
│   ├── chat_completions.R   # generic chat_completions() provider smoke tests
│   ├── gemini.R             # chat, tools, embed, list_models
│   ├── perplexity.R         # chat, streaming, citations, search_recency_filter
│   ├── ellmer.R             # chat_ellmer (anthropic + openai), ellmer_tool(), claude_websearch()
│   ├── openrouter.R         # chat, streaming, multi-turn, tools, list_models, fallback routing
│   ├── llamacpp.R           # chat, streaming, multi-turn, tools, json_schema, logprobs, model management
│   └── ...                  # one file per provider
├── features/                # Opt-in suites for paid/stateful or slow features
│   ├── gemini_grounding.R   # .grounding_threshold, groundingMetadata in api_specific
│   ├── gemini_file_api.R    # upload/chat/metadata/list/delete — on.exit cleanup
│   ├── claude_file_api.R    # same lifecycle for Claude Files API
│   ├── openai_file_api.R    # same lifecycle for OpenAI Files API
│   ├── inline_media.R       # img(), audio_file(), pdf_file(), multi-image — cross-provider
│   ├── perplexity_deep_research.R  # deep_research() blocking + background; slow (5-10 min)
│   └── openai_deep_research.R      # OpenAI o4-mini-deep-research; slow (5-30 min)
├── async_state/             # Gitignored — live batch job objects (.rds)
├── debug_state/             # Gitignored — dump.frames() post-mortem dumps (.rda)
└── results/                 # Gitignored — JSON results for Claude Code to read
    └── latest_run.json      # Written by llt_report() after each run

Running tests: source("local_tests/providers/claude.R") to run a single provider suite. Feature tests: source("local_tests/features/gemini_file_api.R") or run_feature_tests(). Async batch workflow: source("local_tests/batch_submit.R") → wait → source("local_tests/batch_fetch.R"). Claude Code reads: local_tests/results/latest_run.json to see pass/fail state. llama.cpp servers: bash local_tests/start_llamacpp_servers.sh — starts chat (port 8080) and embed (port 8081) servers; idempotent, skips ports already in use.

Debugging failed tests

R’s browser() requires a TTY and cannot be driven from Claude Code. Use post-mortem debugging instead — the harness writes two artifacts for every failure:

  1. Inline traceback in latest_run.json — read this first, usually enough.
  2. dump.frames() dump at local_tests/debug_state/{test_name}.rda — full call stack
    • all local variables captured before stack unwinding. Query any frame with:
    Rscript -e 'load("local_tests/debug_state/my_test.rda"); ls.str(saved_frames[[3]])'
Rscript -e 'load("local_tests/debug_state/my_test.rda"); print(saved_frames[[3]]$response)'

When developing a new feature, run the relevant provider suite after each significant change and read latest_run.json before proceeding. Don’t wait until the end to find out something is broken.

See local_wip/notes/local_testing_strategy.md for full design.

Do not add mocking-based tests without consulting the user first. When adding a new feature, create or extend local_tests/providers/{provider}.R using llt_test() wrappers.

Mocked Tests (tests/testthat/)

Exist for basic provider integration. Mock responses stored in provider-named subdirectories. Use httptest2::with_mock_dir().

Provider Multimodal Capability Matrix

Provider Images Multi-image PDFs Audio Video Notes
claude() Yes, Claude 3+ Yes (up to 600) Yes, native document block No No Files API for upload/reuse; 5MB/img
gemini() Yes, all models Yes (up to 3600) Yes, inline or File API Yes, inline or File API Yes, inline/File API/YouTube File API expires after 48h
openai() Yes, gpt-4o+ Yes Via Files API only (upload_file) Audio-specific models only (gpt-4o-audio-preview) No Files API accepts 80+ formats (images, Office, code, PDF, zip…); input_file in Responses API = documents only; images must use inline img()
openrouter() Yes Yes Text path (binary PDF = text fallback) Yes, model-dependent Yes, model-dependent input_audio/video_url blocks; growing list of AV-capable models: Gemini, Gemma 4, Qwen 3.6, and others — filter by audio modality on openrouter.ai/models
chat_completions() Yes (OAI format) Yes Text path only No No Base class for 7 providers
ollama() Yes, vision models Yes Text path only No No Requires vision GGUF + model pull
groq() Yes, llama-4-scout only Yes (max 5) No No No 4MB base64 cap; vision in preview; separate transcription endpoint
mistral() Yes, most models Yes OCR endpoint only Yes, Voxtral models only No image_url and input_audio are plain strings (not objects); max ~20 min audio
perplexity() Yes, some models Likely No No No Vision under-documented
deepseek() No No No No No Text-only API
llamacpp() Yes, with mmproj Yes No Yes, Ultravox / Qwen2.5-Omni / Qwen3-Omni / Gemma 4 E2B/E4B GGUFs No Audio merged May 2025 (PR #13714); needs --mmproj audio encoder flag; Gemma 4 audio up to 30s
azure_openai() Yes (inherits ChatCompletions) Yes Text path only No No

Inline vs. text-extracted PDFs: pdf_file() always extracts text at construction time as a fallback. Providers listed as “text path only” automatically use this fallback; no special handling needed. Providers with native PDF support (Claude, Gemini) use binary encoding instead.

Audio/video format details: Gemini supports WAV/MP3/AIFF/AAC/OGG/FLAC for audio; MP4/MPEG/MOV/AVI/WebM for video (inline up to 20MB/100MB; larger via File API). OpenRouter forwards input_audio and video_url blocks to the underlying model — audio/video capable models are a growing list including Gemini, Gemma 4 (E2B/E4B), Qwen 3.6, and others; filter by modality at openrouter.ai/models. tidyllm always sends the object format {data, format} for input_audio via OpenRouter (not plain string).

Audio wire format differences: Gemini and OpenRouter use {data: "<b64>", format: "mp3"} objects inside input_audio. Mistral uses a plain string ("<b64>" or URL). llama.cpp uses the same object format as OpenAI/OpenRouter.

tidyllm implementation status: Audio is currently implemented for gemini() and openrouter(). Mistral and llama.cpp audio support is planned for an upcoming 0.5.x release — the wire formats are known and both providers already carry the format difference in their image implementations.

Known Limitations

  • No async/await: Intentional — batch APIs are tidyllm’s preferred async pattern.
  • Ollama default models require pull: Default chat (qwen3.5:4b) and embed (qwen3-embedding:0.6b) require ollama pull qwen3.5:4b && ollama pull qwen3-embedding:0.6b.
  • .imagefile deprecated in 0.5.0: Use .media = img(path) instead. Emits a deprecation warning.
  • .file_ids / .fileid deprecated in 0.5.0: Use upload_file(provider()) + .files on llm_message() instead.
  • OpenAI Files API image limitation: upload_file(openai()) accepts images but the Responses API only supports document types (input_file blocks); use inline img() for OpenAI image vision.
  • Cross-provider chains with audio/video: Audio/video content blocks are silently ignored by providers that don’t support them. When chaining Gemini (audio leg) → Claude (text continuation), the audio item in the prior message is dropped at to_api_format() time — Claude only sees the text content.

Release Status

Current release: 0.4.0 (submitted to CRAN 2026-03-17)

In development: 0.5.0

Phase 1 (OpenAI Provider Split) is complete: - api_chat_completions class (renamed from api_openai); chat_completions() provider for any OAI-compatible endpoint - api_openai rewrote on the Responses API (POST /v1/responses); all generics implemented - OpenAI deep_research() via background=true; check_job()/fetch_job() provider-aware dispatch - .stateful = TRUE on openai_chat() for server-side context via previous_response_id - Built-in tools: openai_websearch(), openai_code_interpreter() (openai_file_search() omitted: queries vector stores, which tidyllm cannot yet manage)

Phase 2 (Files and Media System) is complete: - Four new S7 media types in R/media.R: tidyllm_audio, tidyllm_video, tidyllm_pdf, tidyllm_file - Constructors: audio_file(), video_file(), pdf_file() (lazy encoding); img() unchanged (eager) - llm_message() gains .media and .files; .imagefile and .pdf deprecated with warnings - format_message() returns images list (multi-image); all providers updated to iterate it - add_message() gains .files parameter; print.LLMMessage shows S7 media labels and file refs - Four new file verbs: upload_file(), list_files(), file_info(), delete_file() - validate_message_attachments() called by chat() for provider mismatch detection - Claude: upload_file() returns tidyllm_file; file verbs registered; to_api_format() handles multi-image, PDF text fallback, and message$files - Gemini: same; columns normalized to file_id/filename/size_bytes/mime_type/created_at/expires_at/uri - OpenAI Responses API: upload_file() returns tidyllm_file; to_api_format() emits input_image and input_file blocks - ChatCompletions: to_api_format() updated for multi-image; no Files API (errors with message) - Ollama, Ellmer: to_api_format() updated to iterate formatted$images - OpenRouter: to_api_format() handles tidyllm_audio (input_audio block) and tidyllm_video (video_url block) in addition to images - Live tests: local_tests/features/claude_file_api.R, gemini_file_api.R, openai_file_api.R, inline_media.R (video + cross-provider chain included)

Planned Architecture Changes (0.5.0 — remaining)

  • Mixedbread provider: embedding + quantization, Wholembed v3 omnimodal.
  • Mistral thinking (.thinking → magistral-medium-latest), Mistral OCR (mistral_ocr()).
  • Ollama native structured output: override to_api_format() to use format field directly.
  • Claude Citations API: citations: {enabled: true} on document content blocks.
  • provider_capabilities() discovery function.

Key Environment Variables

Provider Env Var
OpenAI OPENAI_API_KEY
Claude ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
Gemini GOOGLE_API_KEY
Mistral MISTRAL_API_KEY
Groq GROQ_API_KEY
Perplexity PERPLEXITY_API_KEY
DeepSeek DEEPSEEK_API_KEY
Voyage VOYAGE_API_KEY
Azure OpenAI AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY + AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT
Mixedbread MXBAI_API_KEY
OpenRouter OPENROUTER_API_KEY
llama.cpp LLAMACPP_API_KEY (optional) + LLAMACPP_SERVER + LLAMACPP_MODEL_DIR

Internal State

Global state is deliberately minimal: - .tidyllm_rate_limit_env: Per-provider rate limit tracking (updated from response headers) - .tidyllm_stream_env: Buffer for streaming responses

Reference

  • local_wip/notes/workplan_0.4.0.md — Completed 0.4.0 workplan (all phases done; see for full history)
  • local_wip/notes/workplan_0.5.0.md — 0.5.0 workplan: Phase 1 done (Responses API); Phase 2 (files/media) in design
  • local_wip/notes/design_files_and_media.md — Design for Phase 2: tidyllm_file, .media, file verbs, deprecation
  • local_wip/notes/design_openai_stateful.md — Design rationale for .stateful = TRUE on openai_chat()
  • local_wip/notes/TODOs.md — Active feature TODOs and planned work
  • local_wip/notes/tech_debt.md — Known tech debt, ranked by priority
  • local_wip/notes/documentation_ideas.md — Missing vignettes and doc improvements
  • local_wip/notes/local_testing_strategy.md — Full local test design: harness, async tracker, per-provider suites
  • local_wip/notes/features/ — Provider-specific API feature notes (dated); old notes in archive/
    • 2026_03_19_openai.md — Responses API vs Chat Completions split, multimodal, reasoning
    • 2026_03_19_claude.md — Multimodal wire formats, Files API, Citations API
    • 2026_03_19_gemini.md — Full multimodal (image/audio/video/PDF), File API, thinking, grounding
    • 2026_03_19_mistral.md — Vision models, OCR endpoint, audio transcription
    • 2026_03_19_groq.md — Vision (llama-4-scout only), limits, OAI-compat differences
    • 2026_03_19_perplexity.md — Model IDs, vision support, deep research, search features
    • 2026_03_19_deepseek.md — Text-only API, reasoning, context caching
    • 2026_03_19_ollama.md — Native vs OAI-compat image format, vision models, structured output
    • 2026_03_19_llamacpp.md — OAI-compat vision, mmproj flag, vision-capable GGUFs
    • 2026_03_19_openrouter.md — Unified multimodal (image/PDF/audio/video), reasoning, block formats
    • 2026_03_14_mixedbread.md — New provider: embedding + quantization, Wholembed v3 omnimodal
    • 2026_03_14_openrouter.md — New provider: OAI-compat, 100s of models, variant suffixes
    • 2026_03_14_llamacpp.md — New provider: best local server, grammar constraints, embeddings
    • 2026_03_14_tool_use_refactor.md — Design for deduplicating multi-turn tool loop across providers
  • local_tests/media/ — Test media files (images, audio, video, PDF) — tracked in git, co-located with tests
  • local_wip/test_data/ — RDS fixtures for batch and logprobs testing
  • local_wip/feature_dev/ — Active WIP scripts for new features
  • local_wip/presentation/ — Conference demo material
  • vignettes/tidyllm.Rmd — Main user documentation
  • NEWS.md — Changelog
